commit.message:
fix: load correct tree after deleting active chart

When deleting the active chart, handleDeleteChart() was not calling
onChartSwitch() with the new active chart. This left OrgStore holding
the deleted chart's tree, so the remaining chart displayed stale data.

Now checks if the deleted chart was active, and after deletion calls
onChartSwitch() with the new active chart to update OrgStore.

commit.message:
fix: preserve level and dottedLine in normalizeTreeText()

normalizeTreeText() manually constructs OrgNode objects but was not
copying the level or dottedLine fields. This caused levels mapped
during CSV import to be silently dropped when text normalization
(Title Case, Uppercase, Lowercase) was applied.

Added explicit copying for both fields, matching the pattern used
by filterVisibleTree() and cloneBase() in tree.ts.

commit.message:
Add fuzzy case/accent-insensitive name matching for OCR member identification

Replace all 6 exact-substring name comparisons with _names_match() which:
- Normalizes to lowercase and strips accents (é→e, ô→o, etc.)
- Checks substring containment first
- Falls back to SequenceMatcher fuzzy ratio (≥80% threshold)

This correctly matches 'MARYLINE LAMARCHE' to 'Marylin Lamarche' (97% similar)
and 'HÉLOÏSE CÔTÉ' to 'Heloise Cote'.

Applies to both scan session review and single-receipt review paths.

Add callgrind-b4c CI job to profile G4OCCTSolid navigation call distribution (#203)

* Add callgrind-b4c CI job to profile G4OCCTSolid navigation call distribution

B4c uses G4OCCTSolid for the absorber and gap shapes (STEP-imported), making
it the ideal vehicle for profiling realistic G4OCCTSolid call patterns across
all five navigation methods: Inside(p), DistanceToIn(p,v), DistanceToOut(p,v),
DistanceToIn(p) [safety], DistanceToOut(p) [safety].

Changes:

src/examples/B4c/CMakeLists.txt:
- Add option(B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS ON): allows CI to pass OFF to prevent placement
  overlap checking from inflating Inside() call counts during construction.
- target_compile_definitions: B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=<1|0> passed to the compiler
  so DetectorConstruction.hh can use it as a default member initializer.
- if(VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR): add HAVE_VALGRIND_CALLGRIND_H + include path for
  exampleB4c, enabling CALLGRIND macros in RunAction.

src/examples/B4c/include/DetectorConstruction.hh:
- fCheckOverlaps default initializer changed from true to B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS
  so the CI profiling job disables it at compile time without touching source.

src/examples/B4c/src/RunAction.cc:
- Add CALLGRIND guard block (HAVE_VALGRIND_CALLGRIND_H) at top.
- BeginOfRunAction: CALLGRIND_START_INSTRUMENTATION + CALLGRIND_TOGGLE_COLLECT
  -> geometry construction and physics init excluded from profile.
- EndOfRunAction:   CALLGRIND_TOGGLE_COLLECT + CALLGRIND_STOP_INSTRUMENTATION.

src/examples/B4c/run_profile.mac (new):
- 100 events, e- 300 MeV, /run/verbose 0.  Enough statistics for stable
  per-method call ratios; with --instr-atstart=no, runtime is manageable.

.github/workflows/ci.yml:
- New callgrind-b4c job: builds exampleB4c with B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=OFF and
  VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR, runs run_profile.mac under callgrind with gated
  instrumentation, annotates with callgrind_annotate, uploads artifact
  callgrind-reports-b4c (annotated txt + raw .out, 7-day retention).

commit.message:
test: per-fixture distance_tolerance_mm for twisted-swept B-spline loft approximations (#195)

The five twisted-swept fixtures (G4TwistedBox, G4TwistedTrd, G4TwistedTrap,
G4TwistedTubs, G4VTwistedFaceted) use a 64-section polynomial B-spline loft to
approximate the true helicoid lateral faces.  The maximum surface deviation of
this approximation from the analytical helicoid is ~0.10–0.18 µm (shape
dependent), computed as (Δz)²·ω²·r_max/8 where Δz = section spacing, ω = twist
rate in rad/mm, and r_max = maximum corner radius.

The default Geant4 surface tolerance (~1 nm) is far below this error band,
causing spurious DTI/DTO distance mismatches and hard kInside/kOutside
classification errors when near-surface test points (offset by 1 nm from the
native surface) land on the wrong side of the loft surface.

This commit adds infrastructure for per-fixture distance tolerance overrides:

* **provenance.yaml schema**: add optional `validation.distance_tolerance_mm`
  that specifies the accepted approximation error for a fixture.  Set to
  1.0e-3 mm (1 µm, ~7× safety margin) for all five twisted-swept fixtures.

* **CompareFixtureRays**: reads `validation.distance_tolerance_mm` from
  provenance after parsing, overriding `local_summary.distance_tolerance`
  (used for DTI/DTO |Δdistance| checks) when the key is present.

* **CompareFixtureInside**: reads the same key and uses it as the offset
  magnitude in `GenerateNearSurfacePoints` instead of the global surface
  tolerance.  This places test points clear of the approximation error band
  so that points intended to be just inside/outside the native surface are
  also inside/outside the loft surface.  Records the effective tolerance in
  the new `FixtureInsideComparisonSummary::surface_tolerance` field.

G4ExtrudedSolid is exact (generated by DRAWEXE prism) and carries no override.

commit.message:
Replace faceted STEP approximations with exact analytic surfaces for G4Hype and G4Paraboloid (#189)

* Initial plan

* fix: replace faceted lofts with exact analytic NURBS for G4Hype and G4Paraboloid

Replace the 24-segment faceted circular loft STEP files for G4Hype and
G4Paraboloid with exact analytic NURBS solids built from true conic
sections, and remove the corresponding xfail.

The old approach used polyline sections through profile samples fed into
thrusections. The resulting polyhedral surfaces were fundamentally
incompatible with the sub-nanometre ray-comparison tolerance.

New approach (DRAWEXE):
- G4Paraboloid: parabola() with focal F=k/4=1.8 (from r²=7.2·(z+25)),
  trim to t∈[6,18], revol 360° around Z, sew + mkvolume + nurbsconvert.
- G4Hype: hyperbola() with a=8, b=8/tan(20°), trim to u∈[±asinh(25/b)],
  revol 360° around Z, sew + mkvolume + nurbsconvert.

nurbsconvert converts the SURFACE_OF_REVOLUTION (PARABOLA/HYPERBOLA)
to degree-(2,2) RATIONAL_B_SPLINE_SURFACE — the same format used by
the G4Ellipsoid fixture — which the OCCT STEPControl_Reader can read.

The resulting surfaces are geometrically exact: ray comparisons pass at
the Geant4 surface tolerance (≈0.5×10⁻⁹ mm). Volume expectations are
updated to the analytic values; tolerance is 1.0 mm³ to accommodate
BRepGProp Gauss-quadrature integration variance over NURBS.

commit.message:
Remove 117 redundant tests identified by coverage fingerprinting

Per-test instrumented coverage analysis revealed that 715 of 855 tests
cover zero unique Rust source lines. Within that set, 119 tests share
identical coverage fingerprints with another test in the same class —
meaning they exercise the exact same code paths with trivially different
inputs.

Two tests (get_missing_with_default for Document and DictProxy) were
restored after discovering they cover a distinct closure/region within
a shared line — a case where line-level coverage misses sub-line
branching.

Methodology:
- Built instrumented .so with RUSTFLAGS="-Cinstrument-coverage"
- Ran each of 855 tests individually, collecting per-test (file, line) sets
- Grouped tests by identical coverage fingerprint
- Within each same-class group, kept one representative and removed the rest
- Verified: 738 tests pass, line coverage 98.38% (unchanged)

commit.message:
fix: use path-agnostic gcovr filters to fix zero coverage report (#190)

The previous filter anchored on the absolute path returned by
realpath(1) inside the Singularity container:

  --filter "${ROOT}/src/G4OCCT.*"
  --filter "${ROOT}/include/G4OCCT/.*"

Inside the eic-shell Apptainer container the workspace is accessible
via a bind-mount whose path may not round-trip through realpath(1)
identically to the path that cmake (which calls realpath(3) internally
via cmSystemTools::CollapseFullPath) records in .gcno files.  When the
two diverge, gcovr emits "All coverage data is filtered out" and the
report shows 0 / 0 for every metric.

Replace the absolute-path filters with path-agnostic patterns that
start with / (so gcovr treats them as absolute-path regexes) but use
.* to absorb whatever prefix the build system used:

  --filter '/.*/src/G4OCCT[^/]*'
  --filter '/.*/include/G4OCCT/.*'

These match project library source and header files regardless of the
concrete absolute path in use inside the container.  The --root option
(which only controls how paths are displayed in the HTML report, not
which files are included) continues to use realpath(1).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add contextual Tips & Tricks section for remaining daily protein goal (#7)

Users had no in-app guidance on how to close the gap between their
current intake and daily protein goal. This adds a dynamic tips section
that surfaces food suggestions calibrated to the remaining amount.

### Changes

- **`index.html`** — New `#tips-section` container placed below Today's
Log; hidden by default via `display:none`
- **`app.js`**
  - `getTips(remaining)` — returns 3 contextual tips across 4 tiers:
    | Remaining | Suggestions |
    |-----------|-------------|
    | ≥ 60g | Chicken breast 200g, tuna + shake combo, meal planning |
    | 30–59g | Whey shake, tuna 100g, Greek yogurt + cottage cheese |
    | 10–29g | Greek yogurt 150g, two eggs, cottage cheese 100g |
    | < 10g | Milk 200ml, one egg, cottage cheese 50g |
- `renderTips()` — shows/hides section based on remaining; hidden once
goal is met
- `render()` now calls `renderTips()` so tips update live on every entry
change
- **`style.css`** — `.tip-item` uses a green left-border accent
(`border-left: 3px solid var(--green-500)`) and reuses existing
surface/text-muted variables; consistent with the log item design
